excerpt: யாரென்ன சொன்னாலும் நீ யார் என்று அறிவேன்

sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
யாரென்ன சொன்னாலும் நீ யார் என்று அறிவேன்
சீர்மிகு சிற்றம்பலம் அமர் சிவகாமியே
sections.translit:
yArenna sonnAlum nI yAr endRu aRivEn
sIrmigu chitRambalam amar sivakAmiye
sections.en: Whatever others may say about you, I do know who you are Goddess Sivakami seated in the famous Chidambaram shrine.

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
காமனை எரித்த கயிலையர் கனல் சக்தியும் நீ
ராமனாய் அவதரித்த ரம்யை நீ மரகதவல்லி
sections.translit:
kAmanai erittha kayilaiar kanal sakthiyum nI
rAmanAi avadharittha ramyai nI maragathavalli
sections.en: You are the source of the energy of the flame that emanated from Siva, the cause of burning the demigod of love, Manmatha. You are the beautiful goddess Maragathavalli who took the form of Sri Rama.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
வேதார வித்தும் நீ ஆதாரமெதற்கோ அசுரரை வென்ற மோகினி நீ
ராதாகிருஷ்ணன் முன் தோன்றி மறைந்த மாயை நீ
சங்கரன் இடம் உறைந்திடும் நாராயணனும் நீ
சர்வமும் நீ மங்களம் நல்கிடும் நங்கையர் நிதியே
sections.translit:
vEdhAra vitthum nI AdhAramedhaRkO asurarai vendRa mogini nI
rAdhAkrishnan mun thOndRi maRaindha mAyai nI
sankaran idam uRaindhidum nArAyaNanum nI
sarvamum nI mangaLam nalgidum nangaiyar nidhiyE
sections.en: You are the source of all scriptures. Why does anyone need evidence? You are the enchanting Mohini who vanquished the demons. You are the illusory girl born before Krishna (Radhakrishnan) and just disappeared (instilling fear by announcing that the death of demon Kamsa is marked already). You are the Narayana dwelling as part of Sankara. You are everything in the universe and treasure of all women and only one who can bestow everything auspicious. Author’s Note This song reflects extreme advaitic philosophy propagated by Mahaperiyava in his discourses. His detailed description of ambal in different avatarams, Vishnu sitting with Sankara in the position of Parvathy as Sankara Narayana, the worship of the trinity who prostrate before ambal all point to the fact that she is supreme force or sakthi creating, protecting and degrading the world. For each of the avatarams, Mahaperiyava has provided references from Upanishaths, puranas and other divine slokas. For details see:
